lesson: For hlscreen, audit Hyperliquid integration against public read-only Info and WebSocket docs and keep exchange/order/signed-action surfaces out of v1.

For market-data screeners, duplicate public trades must be idempotent by exchange trade identity and rolling features must use decision-time timestamp windows; otherwise replay/reconnect duplicates and stale full-history windows create false market-state confidence.

All-symbol Hyperliquid spot live runs need asset-context receive timestamps to refresh staleness for inactive markets; otherwise a real feed can look stale despite fresh public frames. Keep subscription count under the 1000/IP cap and record gaps explicitly instead of pretending backfill happened.
commands: sqlite3 /tmp/hlscreen-allpairs-15m-hardening-20260708-093507/hls.sqlite "select run_id, clean_shutdown, gap_count from runs"

lesson: Trading-adjacent market-data UIs should label scores as screen heuristics at the point of use and show read-only constraints in the same surface as rankings, so operator polish does not turn into implicit trade advice.

Before adding microstructure rankings, define confidence, score-breakdown, benchmark, and metrics contracts as separate read-only primitives; this prevents later liquidity or why-ranked features from hiding data gaps or using high-cardinality monitoring labels.
commands: cargo test -p hls-core --test confidence_state --test score_breakdown --test metrics_contract

lesson: For live market-data workstations, row confidence belongs in the shared feature snapshot and must be visible beside rank/score; sparse windows, duplicate events, gaps, parser drops, and writer backlog should degrade data quality without becoming trading advice or a hidden execution gate.

Replay parity should compare persisted confidence snapshots against recomputed replay rows and fail closed on drift; this catches data-quality regressions such as reconnect gaps or sparse windows becoming silently trusted.

BBO-only microstructure screens should refuse false precision: keep single-quote or sparse windows as unknown, call OFI/adverse-selection fields proxies, require fresh confidence before tradeability becomes tradeable, and show the caveat in the same TUI pane as the ranking.

Explainability panes for trading-adjacent screens must show component evidence, unavailable evidence, confidence penalties, and caveats beside the ranking; this keeps score polish from becoming implicit trade advice or hidden execution confidence.

Venue metadata tags such as new_listing and fresh_liquidity are discovery context only; keep them optional, mark missing public token details as unknown_metadata, and render the caveat beside the row so metadata polish does not become implicit listing quality or trade advice.

For trading-adjacent OSS tooling, benchmark hashes and metrics contracts are safety controls: use public fixtures only, avoid symbol/run/account metric labels, and reject plugin network/filesystem/private/trading permissions before considering any extension runtime.
